pr.title: Add Linux server gotchas and bwrap sandbox failure docs
pr.body:
## What

Four new verified known issues for Codex CLI on Linux servers, from first-hand experience running Codex exec on an Ubuntu homelab server (April 2026).

## New known issues

### 1. Default bwrap sandbox fails on many Linux servers
- **Severity: High** — exec mode silently hangs or errors with no useful output
- bwrap fails with `RTM_NEWADDR: Operation not permitted` on VPS/homelab machines without `sys_admin` capability
- Fix: `--dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox`

### 2. Old CLI versions silently fail on newer models (gpt-5.5)
- **Severity: High** — v0.27.0 and earlier hang for 10+ minutes or return 400 with no output
- Fix: upgrade to 0.125.0+, smoke-test before long runs

### 3. Codex OAuth token can't be used with raw OpenAI Python SDK
- **Severity: Medium** — `~/.codex/auth.json` tokens are scoped to Codex CLI
- Returns 403/500 when used with `openai` Python SDK
- Fix: use `codex exec` with `--output-last-message` or separate API key

### 4. Long prompts via CLI argument can hang
- **Severity: Medium** — pipe via stdin instead for prompts over ~4K chars
- Fix: `cat prompt.md | codex exec ... -`

## Also added

- **Linux Server Quickstart** section in `guides/automate-cli.md` with the recommended flag pattern and timeout protection
- Three new entries in the **Critical Gotchas** section of `SKILL.md`

## Files changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `skills/codex-cli/reference/known-issues.md` | +115 lines: 4 new verified issues with workarounds |
| `skills/codex-cli/guides/automate-cli.md` | +41 lines: Linux Server Quickstart section |
| `skills/codex-cli/SKILL.md` | +14 lines: 3 new Critical Gotchas entries |
pr.author: pbans-agent
